info.title: MOCA API
info.description:
The unified public API of the **Museum of Crypto Art** — collections, artworks (with original-ratio media), 3D exhibition rooms, and the **Art DeCC0s** knowledge base, aggregated behind a single API key.

All endpoints (except the index) require a MOCA API key sent as `X-API-Key: <key>` or `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

Responses use the Directus-style envelope: `{ "data": …, "meta": … }` on success and `{ "errors": [{ "message", "extensions": { "code" } }] }` on failure.

tags.name: Artworks
tags.description: Artworks across all museum collections, with normalized media

tags.name: DeCC0s
tags.description: The Art DeCC0s knowledge base, aggregated from api.decc0s.com

tags.name: Search
tags.description: Unified search across the whole museum

tags.name: Library
tags.description: The MOCA Library — RAG Q&A and search over the museum's knowledge base (powered by Cortex)

tags.name: Souls
tags.description: Signed agent identity documents (SOUL files) for NFTs, served by Soulweaver — built for ERC-8004 / ERC-8183 / ERC-8257 integrations

tags.name: Presence
tags.description: Ephemeral presence for the Library — public, nothing persisted server-side

get.description: Service information and the list of available endpoints. The only route that works without an API key.
All published top-level museum collections, each with its child collections.

data.name: Genesis Collection
data.title: The Genesis Collection
data.description: The founding artworks of the museum.

get.description: Paged artworks across the museum. Media is normalized server-side: dead URLs are revived, OpenSea's square-cropped CDN variants are swapped for the **original files**, and `ratio` only ever derives from trustworthy dimensions — render `media.url` at `ratio` and you are showing the artwork the way the artist made it.

parameters.name: collection
parameters.description: Comma-separated collection slug(s) to scope the query.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Match against artwork title or artist name.

get.description: The museum's 3D exhibition architecture. Each room ships a GLB model (`model_url`) whose `Slot_001…Slot_NNN` placeholder meshes mark wall positions for hanging artworks — the same convention the museum's own world builder uses.

The 10,000 Art DeCC0 entities, aggregated live from the DeCC0s knowledge base (api.decc0s.com) with a short server-side cache. List responses return a lightweight field set; use `fields` to choose your own, or fetch `/v1/decc0s/{id}` for the full record.

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: Full-text search across the knowledge base.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated field selection (the heavyweight `agent_profiles` / `moltbot` blobs are list-excluded regardless).

get.description: One Art DeCC0 entity by token id (1–10000). Add `include=profiles` for the full agent persona blobs (`agent_profiles`, `moltbot` — hundreds of KB), and `include=codex` to embed the character's codex lore document.
One query across artworks, collections, and DeCC0s — grouped results, capped per group.

post.description: RAG Q&A over the MOCA knowledge base: hybrid retrieval, optional knowledge-graph context, cited sources. For token-by-token output use the streaming variant.

question.description: The question to ask the Library

collection_id.description: Scope to one Library collection (see GET /v1/library/collections)

use_agentic.description: Deep-research mode: multi-step agent reasoning (slower, more thorough)

conversation_history.description: Prior turns for follow-up questions (last 20 kept)

example.question: What is the significance of the Genesis Collection?

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

post.description: Same request as `/v1/library/ask`, answered as **Server-Sent Events**. Each `data:` line is a JSON object: `{"content": "…"}` token chunks, one `{"sources": [...]}` event, optional `{"thinking": "…"}` / `{"sub_questions": [...]}` (agentic mode), `{"graph_context": {...}}`, and a final `{"done": true}`. Deep-research streams can run for minutes — disable proxy buffering.

question.description: The question to ask the Library

collection_id.description: Scope to one Library collection (see GET /v1/library/collections)

use_agentic.description: Deep-research mode: multi-step agent reasoning (slower, more thorough)

conversation_history.description: Prior turns for follow-up questions (last 20 kept)

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

post.description: Hybrid retrieval (vector + keyword + graph) without answer generation — returns scored excerpts. Cheaper and faster than ask; ideal for building your own UX on top.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: The knowledge-base collections available to ask/search against (use their `id` as `collection_id`).

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: Paginated SOUL files generated for a collection's tokens.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: The latest SOUL file for one NFT, including the **EIP-191 verification block** — recover the signer from `verification.message` and compare to `soul.signerAddress` to prove the identity document is authentic. See the [Web3 & agents guide](/web3) for ERC-8004 / ERC-8183 / ERC-8257 integration patterns.

503.description: This surface is not configured on the deployment

get.description: Server-Sent Events feed of Library activity. **Nothing is persisted** — you only receive events broadcast while connected (the docs chat shows this as 'searched since you arrived'). Events: `{"type":"hello","here":n}` on connect, `{"type":"arrived"|"left","here":n}`, and `{"type":"library-search","handle":"…","at":ms}` when someone pings. Questions are never broadcast — only the chosen handle.

post.description: Broadcast the *fact* of a Library search to everyone currently listening — fire it when you ask. Send only a display handle (wallet address, name, or nothing → 'anon'); never the question. Rate-limited per IP.

handle.description: Display handle shown to listeners. Optional.

BearerAuth.description: Your MOCA API key as a bearer token.

Unauthorized.description: Missing, invalid, or revoked API key

RateLimited.description: Rate limit exceeded (default 120 requests/minute per key). Check `Retry-After` and the `X-RateLimit-*` headers.

Upstream.description: The DeCC0s upstream is temporarily unavailable

Media.description: A renderable media variant with a resolved https URL.

url.description: Direct URL (IPFS already resolved to a gateway)

media.description: Best still image — original file preferred over CDN crops

animation.description: Motion variant (video) when the work has one

ratio.description: Trustworthy aspect ratio (width/height); 1 when unknown. Never derived from a square CDN crop.

slots.description: Number of artwork wall slots in the GLB

image_url.description: Preview image (Directus asset URL, supports ?width=&format= transforms)

Decc0Summary.description: Lightweight DeCC0 list item. `*_url` fields are resolved asset URLs added by the aggregation layer.

image_ipfs_url.description: ipfs:// URI of the final artwork

allOf.description: Full knowledge-base record (biography, personality, writing style, geography, …). With `include=profiles` it also carries `agent_profiles` and `moltbot`; with `include=codex` it embeds `codex_document`.

codex_document.description: Only with include=codex; null when no codex file exists for this token

Soul.description: A SOUL file: a portable, signed agent identity document for one NFT.
